content: Asparagus is one of the lowest carbohydrate vegetables, typically containing 4 grams of carbs and 2 grams of fiber per 10-15 grams, providing minimal net effective carbs due to its high prebiotic fiber content.

Cabbage is a cruciferous vegetable rich in sulfur, which can help heal acid reflux and promote gut health. It also contains probiotics, aiding in the establishment of beneficial gut bacteria during ketosis.

Bok choy is a cruciferous vegetable containing indole-3-carbinol and diindolylmethane, which are estrogen modulators. These compounds can help reduce bad estrogens in the body, potentially leading to reduced body fat, especially beneficial for individuals in ketosis.
